evals.prompt: Write the architecture spec for our notification service so a coding agent can build it.
evals.expected_output: A composite doc with context, an arc42/C4-style structure, testable NFRs, and an embedded decision log.
expectations: Includes Context, an architecture/component view, NFRs, and a decision log
Captures decisions with status and rationale
Emits MIF frontmatter type: semantic and passes mif-validate --level 1

evals.prompt: Our architecture doc is just diagrams. What does it need to be implementation-ready?
evals.expected_output: Recommends adding context, NFRs, and a decision log.
expectations: Identifies the missing NFRs and decision log
Explains why diagrams alone are insufficient for an implementer/agent

evals.prompt: How is this different from a plain arc42 doc or a standalone ADR?
evals.expected_output: Explains it composes structure + NFRs + an embedded ADR log into one spec-channel artifact.
expectations: Explains the composite nature (structure + NFRs + decisions together)
Notes arc42-arch-doc and adr are the standalone alternatives
Frames it as the spec-channel one-stop contract
